channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: IAF Set to Finalise Deal for 400 Astra Mk-1 Missiles, Boosting Indigenous BVR Arsenal
item.description: India is on the verge of finalising a major procurement deal for 400 Astra Mk-1 Beyond Visual Range (BVR) air-to-air missiles, significantly strengthening the Indian Air Force’s (IAF) indigenous air combat capabilities. Developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and manufactured by Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL), the Astra Mk-1 represents a key component of India’s push to reduce reliance on imported missile systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF’s Unmanned CSAR Aircraft Program: India Moves Toward Autonomous Combat Rescue Capability
item.description: The Indian Air Force (IAF) is taking a decisive step toward next-generation autonomous warfare systems with the launch of a program to develop an Unmanned Combat Search and Rescue (CSAR) Aircraft. Initiated under the Make-I category of the Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) 2020, the project reflects a clear operational and strategic intent: to reduce human risk in one of the most dangerous mission profiles while strengthening India’s indigenous aerospace capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: UAE Pulls Out of Rafale F5 Funding – France Bears Full Burden, But India’s $40 Billion Order Becomes the Programme’s True Lifeline
item.description: The question on every defence analyst’s mind is clear: will the burden of Rafale F5 development now fall on India after the United Arab Emirates walked away from the programme? The short answer is no. France will ultimately have to finance the Rafale F5 programme alone after the United Arab Emirates decided to withdraw from participation. The dispute centred on access to sensitive technologies, particularly in the field of optronics, which Paris refused to share. Initially, Abu Dhabi had been ready to contribute up to €3.5 billion to a programme estimated at around €5 billion. The collapse of negotiations now leaves France bearing the full cost, with expected delays in development and delivery.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s SSBN Milestone Strengthens Nuclear Triad—Why Long-Range Strategic Bombers Are the Next Critical Step
item.description: India’s induction of its third SSBN (nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine) marks a decisive evolution in its nuclear posture, formally enabling continuous at-sea deterrence. This capability ensures that at least one submarine armed with nuclear-tipped SLBMs remains undetected and operational at all times, significantly enhancing second-strike survivability. With the maritime leg of the nuclear triad now maturing into a credible and persistent deterrent, strategic focus is increasingly shifting toward strengthening the aerial leg—an area where India still lacks a true long-range deep-strike bomber capability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Balances European 6th Gen Fighter Ambitions with AMCA Mk-2 Development Push
item.description: India’s evolving combat aviation strategy is increasingly reflecting a dual-track approach, where participation in a European sixth-generation fighter program is being carefully aligned with the continued development of its indigenous Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) Mk-2. Sources indicate that while New Delhi is actively exploring entry into a European next-generation fighter ecosystem, it has no intention of sidelining its domestic program, which is now being positioned as a bridge between fifth and sixth-generation technologies.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Why India’s MR-SAM Struggles in Exports While South Korea’s Cheongung-II Gains Global Momentum
item.description: India’s Medium Range Surface-to-Air Missile (MR-SAM), co-developed with Israel, represents a technologically capable air defence system with modern radar, network-centric warfare capability, and proven interception performance. However, despite its operational induction with the Indian Armed Forces, the system has struggled to gain traction in the export market. The reasons are not rooted in capability deficits alone, but in a combination of structural, strategic, and commercial factors—many of which stem from the nature of its joint development with Israel and India’s own industrial approach.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Accelerates Deployment of Autonomous Systems in Counter-Terrorism Operations: Robot Dogs, Armed Drones, and Humanoids Take Centre Stage
item.description: India is rapidly transitioning from research and development to real-world induction of autonomous systems, assigning high-risk “dull, dirty, and dangerous” missions to robots and drones. This shift aims to minimise human and animal casualties in counter-terrorism (CT) and counter-insurgency (COIN) operations, particularly in Jammu & Kashmir and other volatile hotspots. The Indian Army has moved beyond conceptual trials, with platforms now appearing in parades, field operations, and dedicated unit structures.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: As Gulf war rages, India readies its third nuclear submarine, with plans for more to follow
item.description: Just before he commissioned INS taragiri, India’s new stealth naval frigate, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh posted a terse and cryptic message on his social media handle – “it’s not a word, it’s power, it’s Aridhaman” – throwing the spotlight on India’s steadily advancing nuclear submarine programme, a cornerstone of its strategic deterrence architecture in an era of missile and drone strikes. INS Arighat, India’s third nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine (SSBN), is currently undergoing sea trials and the post was read by naval analysts that the tests “have been totally successful.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Cul-de-sac in Iran war: Is Pakistan out as mediator and India in as BRICS Chair ?
item.description: Pakistan’s ambitions of mediation between Iran and United StatesIsrael to resolve the current West Asia crisis, specially revolving around Hormuz Strait, have seemingly got bombed as Tehran has reportedly refused to hold talks in Islamabad. Although there has not been any official statement from either Iran, or US-Israel, sources said official Iranian rejections of the process and subsequent reports that Tehran has formally notified mediators of its refusal to participate in talks in Islamabad, have cast a shadow on Pakistan’s role.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India-Flagged LPG Tanker Green Sanvi Crosses Strait Of Hormuz, 2 More In Line
item.description: Another India-flagged liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) tanker, Green Sanvi, transited east of the Strait of Hormuz, according to ship tracking data, making it the seventh Indian vessel to clear the maritime chokepoint safely amid the West Asia conflict.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India rejects payment issues claim over Iranian crude tanker’s diversion to China
item.description: India said on Saturday that there are no payment issues with Iran for crude imports and that refiners continue to source oil from the country, as well as from a wide range of global suppliers. In a post on X, the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas dismissed reports that an oil tanker carrying Iranian crude had rerouted mid-voyage from its previously indicated destination of India, which would have marked the first such shipment in nearly seven years, to China, saying the claims overlooked standard industry practice where cargoes can change destination during transit based on trade optimisation and operational flexibility.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Another India-Bound LPG Tanker Crosses Hormuz Amid Middle East Tensions, Boosts Supply Hopes
item.description: Another India-flagged liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) tanker, Green Sanvi, transited east of the Strait of Hormuz, according to ship tracking data, making it the seventh Indian vessel to clear the maritime chokepoint safely amid the West Asia conflict.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Five Indians among 12 injured in Abu Dhabi missile debris incident
item.description: As many as 12 people, including five Indian nationals, were injured on Friday due to the falling missile debris after a successful interception carried out by UAE’s air defence systems in Ajban area of Abu Dhabi.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Thanks Armenia For Helping Evacuate Citizens From Iran Amid West Asia Tensions
item.description: India on Saturday expressed gratitude to Armenia for facilitating the evacuation of its nationals from Iran, as efforts continue to bring back citizens amid the intensifying conflict in West Asia. The evacuation of Indian fishermen through Armenia marks another step in New Delhi’s ongoing mission to ensure the safe return of its citizens from the region.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Four Valley-Based Insurgents Held In Manipur Amid Intensified Security Crackdown
item.description: Security forces on Friday arrested four dreaded valley-based insurgents from Manipur’s Tengnoupal district, which shares an unfenced border with Myanmar, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin
